name: Andouille in Comforting Barbecue Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the peanut oil in a large pot over medium heat.
2. Add onions and cook until they are clear or tender.
3. Add celery, bell pepper, and parsley; continue cooking until softened.
4. Stir in garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
5. Add steak sauce, Louisiana hot sauce (if using), ketchup, cayenne pepper, and salt to taste.
6. Pour in Southern Comfort liquor.
7. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 2 to 3 hours until thickened.
8. For serving: Slice 1 lb Andouille sausage or smoked sausage into 1/4 inch thick pieces.
9. Combine with about 1 cup of sauce and heat thoroughly on the stovetop or in a chafing dish.
10. Serve over small pieces of French bread or use teethpicks to spear the sausages.
11. Enjoy with plenty of napkins.
